answer:
**In *San Hyan Mahajnana* (translated as "The Sacred Supreme Wisdom"), the term *mahājñāna* ("great knowledge" or "great wisdom") refers to the work's central *tattva* (reality or truth), which is expounded throughout the text as an esoteric Shaivite teaching on consciousness, the subtle body, states of awareness (*jāgrat*, *svapna*, *suṣupti*, *tūrya*), and ultimate union with Shiva.**[^1]

**The specific passage describing what it consists in appears in the concluding verses (84–87), where the Lord Guru instructs Kumāra that *mahājñāna* is the "great secret" (*gūḷya mahat*) eternally present across all states of being. It requires abandoning the manifest (*vyakta*, equated with *prakṛti*) and unmanifest (*avyakta*, equated with *puruṣa*), recognizing Shiva as situated in the *ātma-liṅga* (self-liṅga) and "the void within the void" (*śūnya madhya śūnya*), culminating in *jñānasandhi* (union of wisdom) and liberation (*mokṣa*).**[^2]

[^1]: *San Hyan Mahajnana*, ch. 1, opening: "[p-573696] The Sacred Supreme Wisdom"; key summary in closing: "[p-0f7a95] Thus is the *tattva* of The Sacred Supreme Wisdom, returning to the supreme end." ([/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/))

[^2]: *San Hyan Mahajnana*, ch. 1, ¶¶ [p-6436d4](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-6436d4/), [p-ced8e1](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-ced8e1/), [p-14360c](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-14360c/), [p-f7e6ce](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-f7e6ce/), and [p-ad1274](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-ad1274/). These verses encapsulate: "*In the great wisdom is the great secret... Shiva is situated in the ātma-liṅga, and in the void within the void.*"
